Title: Keyword Matching
Question: Should keyword matching be case-sensitive? 'Ipod' vs 'ipod' appear in the search keywords
Why: Case sensitivity could differ the output.
Possible Assumption: We can assume case insensitivity, since the intent is same

Title: Search Engines
Question: Which search engines are in scope?
Why: We need to do the identify the set of search engines, because query parameter for search query is different. In the input it is mostly q except for yahoo (p)
Possible Assumption: For now, we can assume search engines mentioned in the spec

Title: Search query parameter
Question: Do we have mapping of search engine and search query parameter?
Why: From the input, observed that yahoo search engine is using p as query parameter for search query, where as other search engines are using q.
Possible Assumption: Can hardcode a config map for now — google.com -> q, bing.com -> q, yahoo.com -> p, msn.com -> q. Makes it easy to add new engines later without touching the core logic.

Title: Encoding
Question: URL encoding converts the special, unsafe or non ASCII characters to valid ASCII characters. What kind of character set we can assume for the query string?
Possible Assumption: Can assume UTF-8 and decode all query strings. parse_qs would be able to handle this. Need some more testing.

Title: Sub Domains
Question: Should sub-domains of search engines be treated as valid? e.g. images.google.com
Possible Assumption: For simplicity, we can consider only main domains from the search engine list.

Title: Session
Question: How is a session defined? Do we need to cap the duration between search and purchase?
Why: With the specified input, we can consider only ip or ip with user agent as the session identifier. But there's no session boundary defined, so a search from days ago could still get credited for a much more recent purchase.
Possible Assumption: For simplicity, we can ignore the duration.

Title: Attribution Model
Question: What attribution model should be used? First-touch (search engine that first referred the visitor) or last-touch (most recent search engine before purchase)?
Why: The two models will give completely different results. One can argue that the first touch was the originator. Do we need to split it, or assign it to either first-touch or last touch search engine

Title: Grouping Strategy
Question: Do we need to group by IP and user agent or just by IP to find attribution?
Why: It is possible that person logs in from one browser, search it and add it to cart. Then logs in from another browser and complete the purchase.
Possible Assumption: Group by IP only to start. If it is a really significant pattern in the data, then we can revisit with IP + user agent.

Title: NAT
Question: NAT routers can modify IP address which could be shared by multiple devices.
Why: In big spaces e.g. office or schools, many people could share same IP and the purchase by one user/visitor could be attributed to search by other user/visitor

Title: Zero revenue users/visitors
Question: Should users who came from a search engine but did not purchase still appear in the report?
Possible Assumption: We can ignore these as it may not provide significant value to client and create noise.

Title: Region and Currency
Question: Do we need to consider different regions other than US and currency exchange to get the correct revenue?
Why: The current URLs are not showing currency symbol. It will not reflect correct analysis for different regions and currencies

Title: Joining Condition
Question: Do we need to use the referrer search term to do match (contains) the product name? Or the product list from the event_type 2 (product view)?

Title: hit_time_gmt sorting
Question: Is hit_time_gmt guaranteed to be chronologically sorted within the file?
Why: This will affect the way we do file processing.

Title: Mandatory Fields
Question: How should we handle rows with a null or empty values for mandatory fields? Also, which are optional fields? e.g. event_list and product_list.
Possible Assumption: Treat ip, hit_time_gmt, referrer as mandatory fields

Title: Handle Missing Values
Question: How should we handle missing values? Should we do listwise deletion or average imputation? e.g. What if event_list contains 1 but product_list is empty or has no revenue?

Title: Negative Revenue
Question: Is there any use case of negative revenue? e.g. Refund transactions
Possible Assumption: Assume that this is not a valid case or in this case the revenue will be 0.

Title: SLAs
Question: Do we have any SLA requirements when processing large files?
